xposts.title: Spirituality as mental construction of reality
xposts.excerpt: I have observed that most atheists and most theists take claims about the existence of God to be statements about the construction of [physical/external] reality, instead of the construction of reality in the mind. This confuses even Aquinas and Dawkins. Spirituality is mental.

xposts.title: Holy books as rational state-building manuals
xposts.excerpt: Read holy books and keep in mind: they are ancient primers and manuals for state building. Authors and curators were fully rational, but audience and contemporary interpreters are not. Original intentions got often lost, and based on different value systems and ontologies.

xposts.title: God and spirits as mind-generated agency models
xposts.excerpt: All experience is generated in the mind. A spirit (including your own) is a model of an agency. If God is the spirit of a civilization, and the civilization has stopped to project itself into the future, God is dying, no?

xposts.title: Enlightenment as reconfiguration of self vs experience
xposts.excerpt: Enlightenment (in the spiritual sense) is often understood as state of heightened wisdom and ethics. But enlightenment primarily affects how the individual self constitutes itself with respect to its experiences and motivation. It does not magically make you intelligent or wise.

xposts.title: Science vs secular religion of belief and priests
xposts.excerpt: Science is the organized search for truth, which requires informed doubt. "Belief in science" can turn into a secular religion, where credentialed priests give spiritual guidance in ways that cannot be questioned without risking ostracism. That's incompatible with science.

xposts.title: Ideology, cults, religion as upstream belief control
xposts.excerpt: Ideology emerges over unconditional beliefs, anchored in a sense of good and evil encoded upstream of the self. These beliefs cannot be changed by the host, but they can be manipulated from the outside. Ideologues, cults, religion control the social construction of reality.

xposts.title: Rationality vs ideology: truth before good/evil
xposts.excerpt: In an ideological world view, right/wrong trumps true/false: truth is accepted when it is congruent with the ideology and rejected when it is not; all truth needs to be good. In a rational world view, you cannot know whether you are good or evil if you are not putting truth first.

terms.title: Spirituality
terms.definition: Mental construction of reality and models of agency rather than claims about external physical facts. Involves generating spirits as agency models in the mind, distinct from physicalist ontology.

terms.title: God
terms.definition: A spirit modeled as the agency of a civilization or collective mind; religious descriptions function as mental tools or specifications for read/write access rather than factual ontology.
